musaiumGates.noUnicodeEmoji: PASS — python3 emoji-regex scan over the 5 new sottoVoce keys × 8 locales (40 strings) returns zero matches. The en-dash ('—') used in fr/en/es/it/zh hints is a typographic dash, not an emoji.
musaiumGates.noFeatureFlag: PASS — no `*_ENABLED` flag introduced. `useSottoVoce` exposes a real persisted user-preference (`settings.sotto_voce_mode`), not a flag. Doctrine `feedback_no_feature_flags_prelaunch` respected (R30).
musaiumGates.buryDeadCode: PASS — no DEPRECATED markers, no zombie stub. New code is purely additive (hook + atomic component + 2 optional props on ChatHeader + 1 line gate on screen). N/A for legacy deletion.
musaiumGates.i18nReal: PASS — 5 keys × 8 locales = 40 strings, all with real native-language translations (verified per-locale in §2.6 of spec). `chat.sottoVoce` namespace created in all 8 files (ar/de/en/es/fr/it/ja/zh) ; `grep -c sottoVoce` = 1 per file (single object literal). Zero placeholder English in non-en locales.
musaiumGates.noNewDeps: PASS — `git status` shows no `package.json` / `package-lock.json` mutation. `@react-native-async-storage/async-storage` already installed (used via the shared `storage` façade). NFR7 respected (R5).

tests.summary: Test Suites: 232 passed, 232 total ; Tests: 2410 passed, 2410 total ; Snapshots: 4 passed
tests.delta: +38 vs A4 baseline 2372 — matches B5 expected (~9 + ~9 + ~5 + sibling additions). Spec AC20 estimated ≥25 ; delivered 38 (useSottoVoce ~13, SottoVoceToggle ~15, chat-sotto-voce ~6, plus integration helpers). All green.
tests.workerLeakNote: Pre-existing Jest 'worker process has failed to exit gracefully' warning still present (baseline noise inherited from A4 review — not introduced by B5).

scopedTests.summary: Test Suites: 3 passed, 3 total ; Tests: 38 passed, 38 total ; Time: 1.683 s
scopedTests.note: All 3 red files turn green. One residual `console.debug('[chat.sottoVoce]', { next: true })` log line appears in stdout — emitted intentionally by `<SottoVoceToggle>` onPress telemetry (R17), captured in `at debug (features/chat/ui/SottoVoceToggle.tsx:33:12)`. Not a test failure.

correctness.rationale: All 31 EARS (R1-R31) and 24 ACs (AC1-AC24) satisfied. `useSottoVoce` mirrors `useAudioDescriptionMode` semantics exactly : `SOTTO_VOCE_STORAGE_KEY = 'settings.sotto_voce_mode'` exported (R8/AC1), initial `enabled=false / isLoading=true` (R3/AC3), only the literal string `'true'` is treated as truthy (defensive parsing test at useSottoVoce.test.tsx:107-115 — guards against 'TRUE' false-positive), reject-tolerant (R6/AC6 — `.catch(() => setIsLoading(false))`), cancelled-flag guards post-unmount writes (R7), persistence via `storage.setItem(KEY, String(next))` writes the literal `'true'`/`'false'` (R4/AC5). `<SottoVoceToggle>` exposes `testID='sotto-voce-toggle'` (R11/AC7), icon `mic-off-outline`→`mic-off` (R12/AC7-8), `accessibilityRole='button'` + `accessibilityState.selected` (R13/AC7-8), label/hint i18n keys (R14/AC10-11), telemetry `console.debug('[chat.sottoVoce]', { next: !enabled })` (R17). `<ChatHeader>` renders the toggle iff `onToggleSottoVoce` provided (R19/R20/AC12-13) in the correct order [audio-desc] [sotto-voce] [summary] [close] (R19 — verified ChatHeader.tsx:98-152). Screen gate `effectiveAudioDesc = (sessionAudioOverride ?? audioDescEnabled) && !sottoVoce` (R23/AC16-17) — verified in [sessionId].tsx working-tree diff lines +163-168.
correctness.evidence: useSottoVoce.ts:5 (key) ; useSottoVoce.ts:19-48 (state machine) ; SottoVoceToggle.tsx:28-62 (component) ; ChatHeader.tsx:120-122 (conditional render) ; [sessionId].tsx working-tree diff +25/+163/+406-409 (wire-up + gate) ; 38/38 green tests cover every AC.
minorNotes: AC11 hint comparison test uses the i18n test-utils mock returning the key verbatim — this is the established codebase convention for hint assertions (parity with A3/A6 reviews). The real translations are validated by AC18 (i18n bundle scan).

scope-fidelity.rationale: ZERO modification to `useAutoTts`, `useTextToSpeech`, `useAudioRecorder`, `useVoiceDisclosure`, BE, Expo Router, dep tree, design-system tokens, or feature flags (R29/R30/R31 verified via `git diff --stat` — only B5-named files touched). The gate lives at screen composition level (1 expression change : `&& !sottoVoce`), preserving `useAutoTts({ messages, enabled })` signature verbatim — `useAutoTts.ts` not in the diff. The hook + component are isolated additions ; the `<ChatHeader>` extension is 2 optional props with safe defaults (backward-compat AC13). Locale files gain exactly 5 keys per locale × 8 = 40 strings, no other locale mutation tied to B5. The screen wires `sottoVoceEnabled` + `onToggleSottoVoce` via prop-drilling on `<CollapsibleTopBar>` — no new global store / context (R25).
scope-fidelity.evidence: `git diff --stat` shows only: hook (new), SottoVoceToggle (new), 3 test files (new), ChatHeader.tsx (mod), [sessionId].tsx (mod), 8 locale files (mod), B5 spec + tracking.md (mod). No `package.json`, no BE file, no `useAutoTts.ts`, no shared/ui changes. grep `useAutoTts|useTextToSpeech|useAudioRecorder|useVoiceDisclosure` over the diff = 0 source-file modifications (only test mocks reference them).

kiss-dry-hexagonal.rationale: Hook isolated in `application/` (sibling to `useAudioDescriptionMode`) — identical state machine (2 useState + 1 useEffect + 1 useCallback). Component is a thin React.memo Pressable + Ionicons (45 SLOC pure code, no business logic). Single source of truth for the storage key (`SOTTO_VOCE_STORAGE_KEY` exported constant — R8). Composition over inheritance : the screen does `(... ) && !sottoVoce` rather than embedding the gate inside `useAutoTts` (preserves hook contract). Backward-compat default `sottoVoceEnabled = enabled ?? false` in the conditional render (ChatHeader.tsx:121). No duplication with `useAudioDescriptionMode` despite the mirror — the two prefs are intentionally distinct (one global TTS policy, one session-level mute). The `useCallback(toggle, [enabled])` is correct (depends on the captured `enabled` for the optimistic flip).
kiss-dry-hexagonal.evidence: useSottoVoce.ts (48 SLOC total) ; SottoVoceToggle.tsx (74 SLOC including styles) ; ChatHeader.tsx:120-122 (3-line conditional) ; [sessionId].tsx:+163 gate (1 line change vs A4 baseline).
minorNotes: N1 — `useCallback(toggle, [enabled])` re-creates the callback on every flip. Functionally correct (the optimistic `!enabled` read needs the current value), but if a future regression callback-stabilises with `useRef(enabled)`, refactor accordingly. Acceptable today : the hook caller (screen) wraps it in `() => void toggleSottoVoce()` so the identity churn does not propagate to memoised children.

N2 — `<SottoVoceToggle>` recomputes `handlePress` on every render (not memoised). Trivial (1 closure per render, no listener leak). React.memo on the component still works because the prop identity of `onToggle` is whatever the parent passes — the screen passes a fresh `() => void toggleSottoVoce()` each render which would defeat memo, but the toggle itself only re-renders when `enabled` flips (rare). Sub-cosmetic.

a11y-design-system.rationale: Convention `accessibilityRole='button'` + `accessibilityState.selected` is correct for a Pressable-as-bascule (not `'switch'` which is reserved for native `<Switch>`) — spec §0.6 explicit and tests at SottoVoceToggle.test.tsx:75-79 explicitly assert NOT `'switch'`. Label + hint via i18n (R14) — hint changes between `_on`/`_off` based on state. Hit target 36×36dp via `space['9']` (R15, parity with the 3 existing header action buttons — verified ChatHeader.tsx:202-209). DS tokens reused (`radius.full`, `semantic.input.borderWidth`, `theme.primary`/`theme.textSecondary`/`theme.inputBorder`/`theme.surface`) — ZERO new token (NFR6). Icon size literal 20 matches other Ionicons header actions. The two announcement keys (`announcement_on`/`announcement_off`) ship in the i18n bundle for V1.1 `AccessibilityInfo.announceForAccessibility()` wiring (R28 — Open Q2) — V1 does not call it (honest deferral).
a11y-design-system.evidence: SottoVoceToggle.tsx:37-58 (role + state + labels + hint + icons) ; SottoVoceToggle.tsx:63-73 (styles) ; locale files (5 keys × 8 = 40 strings) ; AC18 i18n bundle scan validated above (40/40 non-empty + zero emoji).
minorNotes: Pas d'animation, donc `useReducedMotion()` n'est pas consulté (R16). Cohérent avec le spec §2.9. iOS Reduce Transparency NO-OP — la palette ne touche pas à BlurView.
V1 ne call pas `AccessibilityInfo.announceForAccessibility()` (Open Q2, R28) — VoiceOver re-annonce de toute façon le `selected` state au flip, donc la redondance manquante n'est pas un blocage a11y.

security-honesty.rationale: Zéro PII en stockage (`'true'`/`'false'` only). Zéro telemetry réseau (`console.debug` local only). Zéro `eslint-disable` added (verified via grep on new files + diff). Zéro `as any` (verified). Zéro TODO/FIXME left (verified). Le commentaire JSDoc dans `useSottoVoce.ts:7-18` documente honnêtement la mirror-pattern et la tolérance read-failure ; `SottoVoceToggle.tsx:14-26` documente le choix de role 'button' vs 'switch' avec rationale RN. Tradeoffs documentés : Q1 ambient detection deferred V1.1 (rationale battery + permission + faux-positifs §0.3), Q2 announceForAccessibility deferred V1.1 (i18n keys déjà prêtes pour zéro modif locale en V1.1), Q3 Sentry breadcrumb deferred V1.1, Q4 haptic deferred. Le `useCallback(toggle, [enabled])` lit `enabled` au moment du toggle — la sémantique 'flip current value' est honnête (pas de fonctional setState dans le hook ; cohérent avec `useAudioDescriptionMode`).
security-honesty.evidence: useSottoVoce.ts:7-18 + 33-35 (reject tolerance comment) ; SottoVoceToggle.tsx:14-26 + 32-33 (telemetry justification) ; grep `eslint-disable|as any|TODO|FIXME` on new files returns 0 ; `npm run lint` exit 0 with `--max-warnings=0`.
minorNotes: N3 — Le `useCallback(toggle, [enabled])` n'utilise pas la forme functional setState (`setEnabled(prev => !prev)`). En cas de double-tap ultra-rapide (avant que React n'ait commit le flip), le second toggle pourrait lire un `enabled` stale. Inhérent à React + Pressable + AsyncStorage write async. Le test 'optimistically' (useSottoVoce.test.tsx:150-164) couvre le single-flip ; un double-tap rapide n'est pas testé. Pratiquement, 36dp + Pressable debounce + un user qui tap deux fois fast n'est pas un scénario qui rend l'UI incorrecte (le state final = même valeur qu'il aurait été — toggle pair). Acceptable sans remédiation. Si on voulait être strict : remplacer par `setEnabled(prev => { void storage.setItem(KEY, String(!prev)); return !prev; })` — mais cela diverge du pattern mirror `useAudioDescriptionMode` (qui a la même 'imperfection' héritée). Cohérence > optimisation.
N4 — Le `console.debug('[chat.sottoVoce]', { next })` apparaît dans le test stdout (lint test-output noise) — cohérent A3/A6 pattern. Pas un bug, pas un fail.

niceToHave.title: `useCallback(toggle, [enabled])` recreates the closure each flip — sub-cosmetic, sibling-pattern parity
niceToHave.evidence: The callback identity changes on every `enabled` flip. The consumer ([sessionId].tsx) wraps it in `() => void toggleSottoVoce()` so the churn is fully absorbed at the call site — `<CollapsibleTopBar>` and `<ChatHeader>` receive a fresh `onToggleSottoVoce` each render but React.memo on `<SottoVoceToggle>` cuts the cascade.
niceToHave.spec-ref: B5.md §2.2 (line 301-305) — spec uses exactly this form.
niceToHave.honest-tradeoff: Mirror parity with `useAudioDescriptionMode` (which has the same pattern). Switching to `setEnabled(prev => …)` would functionally improve double-tap robustness but break the documented mirror invariant. Acceptable.
niceToHave.remediation: Optional V1.1 : refactor BOTH `useAudioDescriptionMode` AND `useSottoVoce` to use functional setState — keeps mirror parity intact. Not required for B5 done.

niceToHave.title: `<SottoVoceToggle>` recreates `handlePress` on every render (not wrapped in useCallback)
niceToHave.evidence: The arrow function is declared inline in the component body and passed to `<Pressable onPress={handlePress}>`. React.memo on the component still works because the component only re-renders when `enabled` flips.
niceToHave.honest-tradeoff: Functionally correct. Pre-launch perf budget : the `<SottoVoceToggle>` re-renders only on user-driven toggle (max 1× per second realistically). Not a hot path.
niceToHave.remediation: Optional V1.1 nano-perf : wrap in `useCallback(handlePress, [enabled, onToggle])`. Not required.

niceToHave.title: Test stdout shows `console.debug('[chat.sottoVoce]', { next })` noise (intentional telemetry, not a bug)
niceToHave.evidence: `fireEvent.press()` invokes the real `console.debug` from `<SottoVoceToggle>`. Captured stack `at debug (features/chat/ui/SottoVoceToggle.tsx:33:12)` confirms the source.
niceToHave.spec-ref: B5.md §R17 — telemetry is required (`console.debug` is the V1 channel, Open Q3 defers Sentry).
niceToHave.remediation: Optional V1.1 : wrap with a `jest.spyOn(console, 'debug').mockImplementation()` in `beforeAll` of the test file to silence the noise. Cosmetic only.

niceToHave.title: Jest emits 'A worker process has failed to exit gracefully' at end of full `npm test`
niceToHave.evidence: Warning still present from prior reviews (A1-A4). Not introduced by B5 — neither `useSottoVoce.test.tsx` nor `SottoVoceToggle.test.tsx` start timers or open handles.
niceToHave.spec-ref: Not a B5 finding — pre-existing baseline noise (documented in A4-review.json:N3).
niceToHave.remediation: Out of B5 scope. Already tracked from A4 review.

T1-ambient-noise-detection-deferred.evidence: Spec §0.3 + Open Q1 explicit: battery drain + mic permission EU AI Act + faux positifs en musée bruyant. V1 manual toggle suffit pour scénario primaire (visiteur entre salle silencieuse, coupe NOW). V1.1 post-bake.

T2-announcement-deferred-keys-shipped.evidence: Spec R28 + Open Q2 : i18n keys `announcement_on/off` livrées en V1 (40 strings → zéro modif i18n en V1.1) mais pas appelées. `accessibilityState.selected` re-annoncé par VO/TalkBack au flip — redondance manquante non bloquante.

T3-mirror-useaudiodescriptionmode.evidence: Hook mirror parfait (state machine + storage key pattern + reject tolerance + cancelled flag). DRY via convention, pas via extraction d'un hook générique (overhead injustifié pour 2 instances).

T4-screen-level-gate-vs-hook-coupling.evidence: Spec §0.7 / R23 : gate `(... ) && !sottoVoce` au screen composition level plutôt qu'embedded dans `useAutoTts`. Préserve la signature `useAutoTts({ enabled })` intacte. `useAutoTts.ts:62-67` déclenche `stopPlayback()` quand `enabled` flips à false — gate naturel R24 (in-flight audio stopped automatically). Acceptable et préservé.

T5-accessibility-role-button-not-switch.evidence: Convention RN documentée spec §0.6 — `accessibilityRole='switch'` est strictement réservé au composant natif `<Switch>`. Pressable-as-bascule utilise `role='button'` + `state.selected`. Test SottoVoceToggle.test.tsx:75-79 garde-fou explicite contre régression.

T6-console-debug-only-no-sentry-v1.evidence: Cohérent A3-A6 pattern. Open Q3 défère Sentry breadcrumb à V1.1 si support remonte des cas (e.g., user toggle 3× / min → UI confusion). Pas de signal réseau pour zéro PII.

gates.musaium-doctrine: PASS — 5/5 (no emoji 40/40 strings, no flag, no dead code N/A, real i18n 5×8, no new deps)
gates.honesty: PASS — known caveats (useCallback non-functional setState, jest worker leak, console.debug stdout noise) reported explicitly ; deferred Q1-Q4 documented in spec + this review

recommandation.rationale: weightedMean 95 ≥ 85 threshold. Zero blocker, zero important finding. 4 nice-to-haves : N1/N2 sub-cosmetic perf (mirror parity preserved), N3 intentional telemetry noise, N4 pre-existing baseline noise (already documented A4). All 31 EARS + 24 ACs covered. Lint + 2410 tests green. 5/5 Musaium gates pass. Honest tradeoffs documented (Q1-Q4 deferred V1.1 with rationale). B5 can flip `review → done` in tracking.md.
recommandation.merge-readiness: Ready. Zero BE delta. Zero new dep. Zero touched core hooks (useAutoTts/useTextToSpeech/useAudioRecorder/useVoiceDisclosure preserved). Backward-compat ChatHeader (omitting `onToggleSottoVoce` hides the toggle entirely).
